pr.title: Match 15 game mode stage data accessor functions
pr.author: malvarezcastillo

summary: Matched a set of small game-mode stage data accessor functions for Adventure, All-Star, and Classic mode, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS stubs with typed u8/f32 accessors over newly described stage-data table entry structs. The PR also matched mnname's IsNameNotAllowed with a bool return and typed CompareNameStrings prototype. Review focused on whether the code looked overly artificial, whether loops/array indexing should be cleaned up, and which source forms were required to preserve matching assembly.
what_changed: Added AdventureStageEntry in gm_17C0.c with size 0x1A, fields stage_kind, scale0_pct, scale1_pct, padding, and STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(AdventureStageEntry) == 0x1A).
Added AllstarStageEntry in gm_17C0.c with size 0x1A, fields stage_kind, scale0_pct, scale1_pct, scale2_pct, scale3_pct, padding, and a static size assertion.

Declared extern AdventureStageEntry lbl_803D7AC0[110] and extern AllstarStageEntry lbl_803D85F0[55], documenting Adventure as 22 stages × 5 difficulty levels and All-Star as 11 stages × 5 difficulty levels.

Matched Adventure mode accessors gm_8017E500, gm_8017E528, gm_8017E578, gm_8017E664, and gm_8017E6B4 using manual index calculation stage_slot + difficulty * 5 and converting percentage fields to f32 ratios by dividing by 100.0F.
Matched All-Star mode accessors gm_8017EB3C, gm_8017EC00, gm_8017EC50, gm_8017ED3C, and gm_8017ED8C over lbl_803D85F0 with the same flattened table indexing pattern.
Updated gm_17C0.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete u8 and f32 signatures for the newly matched Adventure and All-Star accessors.
Added ClassicStageEntry in gm_1832.c with size 0x10, fields stage_kind, scale0_pct, scale1_pct, padding, and STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(ClassicStageEntry) == 0x10).
Declared extern ClassicStageEntry lbl_803D9910[65], documenting Classic as 13 stages × 5 difficulty levels.
Matched Classic mode accessors gm_8018A160, gm_8018A188, gm_8018A1D8, gm_8018A2C4, and gm_8018A314, again using flattened stage_slot + difficulty * 5 indexing and percentage-to-float conversion.
Updated gm_1832.h prototypes for Classic accessors from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed u8/f32 signatures.

Implemented IsNameNotAllowed in mnname.c as a bool-returning loop over NotAllowedNamesList, stopping at mnNameNew_NullCharacter and returning true when CompareNameStrings indicates a disallowed name match.
Updated mnname.h so CompareNameStrings is typed as s32 CompareNameStrings(char* str, s32 slot) and IsNameNotAllowed is typed as bool IsNameNotAllowed(s32 name_idx).
smart_moves: Used narrow, named structs for data-table entries instead of anonymous byte offsets, while preserving exact layout with explicit padding and STATIC_ASSERT size checks.
Documented table dimensions and the flattened indexing formula, making otherwise opaque global data accesses searchable and reviewable.
Renamed generic fields and parameters to meaningful names such as stage_kind, scale0_pct, scale1_pct, difficulty, stage_slot, and name_idx without claiming more semantics than the diff supported.
Kept percentage fields as u16 table values and converted them at accessor boundaries with division by 100.0F, matching the table representation while exposing f32 behavior.
Added an explanatory comment for the (u8) cast on int difficulty in Adventure accessors, tying it to existing function-pointer signatures typed as f32(*)(int, u8) in UnkAdventureData.
Tried reviewer-suggested cleanup such as 2D array indexing, but retained the flattened lookup form when it produced the matching assembly.
Accepted review feedback on IsNameNotAllowed by changing the return type to bool and using true/false instead of 1/0.
decomp_lessons: For small table accessors, a clean struct definition plus explicit flattened index arithmetic can be a good compromise between readability and exact codegen.
Do not automatically rewrite flattened data tables as 2D arrays; in this PR, arr[difficulty][stage_slot] reportedly compiled differently than arr[stage_slot + difficulty * 5].
When a parameter is passed as int through a function pointer but the original code narrows it before indexing, keep the cast and document why it exists rather than hiding it as a cleanup.
Percentage-like table fields may need to remain integer storage fields such as scale*_pct, with f32 division performed in accessor functions to match the binary.
Static size assertions are valuable when introducing guessed or partially understood structs for binary data tables.
Reviewer suspicion that code looks fake can be addressed with decomp.me scratches, comments about codegen constraints, and preserving match-required source forms.
Header prototype updates are part of decomp progress: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete signatures improves future callsite work and reduces ambiguity.
naming_conventions: Entry structs were named by mode and role: AdventureStageEntry, AllstarStageEntry, ClassicStageEntry.
Stage table fields used semantic names based on observed accessor behavior: stage_kind for the u8 stage ID and scale0_pct/scale1_pct/scale2_pct/scale3_pct for u16 percentage fields divided by 100.
Padding was named by offset, e.g. pad_1, pad_6, pad_14, preserving layout clarity without inventing semantics.
Accessor parameters were named difficulty and stage_slot, replacing generic or misleading names such as count/arg0.
mnname parameter naming used name_idx for the selected name slot/index.
Global labels such as lbl_803D7AC0, lbl_803D85F0, and lbl_803D9910 were not renamed, but were given typed extern declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Manual flattened indexing stage_slot + (difficulty * 5) was kept because reviewer-suggested 2D array syntax reportedly changed generated assembly.
Some Adventure accessors use ((u8) difficulty * 5) even though difficulty is int, because callsites/function-pointer types pass int and the original behavior narrows for array indexing.
Accessor functions return table u16 percentage fields as f32 divided by 100.0F, matching ratio-returning behavior.

The discussion notes that source loop shape can affect branch structure; an if+goto/backward branch pattern had been considered for matching IsNameNotAllowed, though the final diff shows a while (true) loop after review.
A decomp.me scratch was shared in review to demonstrate an alternative matching cleanup for the mnname loop issue.
Exact struct sizes were enforced with STATIC_ASSERT to prevent layout drift from breaking data-table access codegen.
review_feedback: r-burns initially said IsNameNotAllowed looked mergeable with cleanup, while the other functions looked fake and should probably be dropped.
r-burns requested that the IsNameNotAllowed loop should be a for loop or otherwise cleaned up from the initial goto-style loop.
r-burns noted IsNameNotAllowed appears to return a bool and suggested bool IsNameNotAllowed(s32 arg0) with return true/false instead of return 1/0.
The author replied that the goto/loop pattern had been used for matching because compiler branch structure differed, but later accepted cleanup after a decomp.me scratch was provided.
r-burns suggested using 2D arrays and u8 args for the stage table lookups, e.g. lbl_803D7AC0[difficulty][stage_slot].stage_kind.
The author reported that 2D array syntax did not match the original assembly and kept the manual calculation stage_slot + difficulty * 5, while preserving struct definitions and clearer names.
r-burns accepted the explanation after the attempt: "Ok appreciate you giving it a try, thanks for humoring my suggestions."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_803D7AC0, lbl_803D85F0, or lbl_803D9910 to semantic table names.
Check whether UnkAdventureData and UnkAllstarData function pointer signatures were later refined to align with these accessor prototypes.
Look for subsequent work on gm_8017E5C8, gm_8017E5FC, gm_8017E630, gm_8017E704, gm_8017EB64, gm_8017EB98, gm_8017EBCC, gm_8017ECA0, gm_8017ECD4, and gm_8017ED08.
Search for later mnname PRs that further clean up IsNameNotAllowed, CompareNameStrings, or NotAllowedNamesList typing.

Investigate whether the table dimensions documented here, especially Adventure's stated 22 stages × 5 difficulty levels versus a 110-entry array indexed by difficulty * 5 + stage_slot, were later clarified or corrected.
